  • Rate limiting — token bucket, leaky bucket, fixed window, sliding window, distributed
  • Token bucket — bursts allowed, fill rate, capacity
  • Leaky bucket — smooth output rate, queue-based
  • Sliding window log — exact count, high memory
  • Sliding window counter — approximation, memory efficient
  • Circuit breaker — CLOSED, OPEN, HALF-OPEN, failure threshold, reset timeout
  • Bulkhead — isolate failures, separate thread pools/resources per dependency
  • Retry with backoff — exponential, jitter, max retries, Retry-After header
  • Timeout — fast fail, prevent cascading, connect timeout vs read timeout
  • Back pressure — producer slows down when consumer overwhelmed
  • Caching — in-process, distributed, CDN, strategies (cache-aside, write-through, write-behind)
  • Cache stampede — probabilistic early expiry, mutex lock, request coalescing
  • Cache invalidation — TTL, event-driven, cache-aside, tag-based
  • CDN — edge caching, Cache-Control, ETag, Last-Modified, purge APIs
  • Proxy — forward proxy (client-side), reverse proxy (server-side), nginx, HAProxy
  • API Gateway — routing, auth, rate limiting, logging, circuit breaking
  • Service mesh — sidecar proxy, mTLS, traffic management, observability
  • DNS — A record, CNAME, TTL, GeoDNS, health check routing
  • Load shedding — drop requests when overloaded, priority queues
  • Graceful degradation — serve reduced functionality when dependencies fail
  • Feature flags — dark launch, canary, A/B testing, kill switch
  • Blue-green deployment — two environments, instant cutover, rollback
  • Canary deployment — gradual traffic shift, metric-gated promotion
  • Database connection pooling — HikariCP, PgBouncer, max pool size, idle timeout
  • N+1 query problem — SELECT in loop, batch fetching, JOIN FETCH, DataLoader
  • Pagination — offset/limit, cursor-based, keyset, seekMethod
  • Search — inverted index, Elasticsearch, relevance scoring, faceting
  • Full-text search — tokenization, stemming, TF-IDF, BM25
  • Message queue — decoupling, async, durability, Kafka, SQS, RabbitMQ
  • Event streaming — ordered log, replay, consumer groups, Kafka
  • WebSocket — full-duplex, persistent, ws://, heartbeat, reconnect
  • Server-Sent Events (SSE) — server push, text/event-stream, one-directional
  • Long polling — client waits, server holds response until data available
  • gRPC — HTTP/2, Protocol Buffers, streaming, bidirectional
  • REST — stateless, uniform interface, HATEOAS, idempotent methods
  • GraphQL — single endpoint, client-specified fields, N+1 with DataLoader
  • CQRS at system level — separate read/write databases, sync via events
  • Saga pattern — choreography vs orchestration, compensation
  • Outbox pattern — transactional message publishing, CDC, Debezium
  • Change Data Capture (CDC) — log-based, query-based, Debezium, Maxwell
  • Two-phase locking (2PL) — shared/exclusive locks, deadlock prevention
  • Optimistic locking — version field, retry on conflict, no lock held
  • Pessimistic locking — SELECT FOR UPDATE, hold lock during transaction
  • MVCC (Multi-Version Concurrency Control) — read snapshot, no read locks
  • Deadlock — detection (wait-for graph), prevention (ordering), avoidance (timeout)
  • Write-ahead log (WAL) — durability, crash recovery, log-structured storage
  • LSM tree — write-optimized, MemTable + SSTable, compaction, RocksDB, Cassandra
  • B-tree — read-optimized, balanced, disk-friendly, PostgreSQL, MySQL InnoDB
  • Bloom filter — probabilistic, no false negatives, space-efficient membership test
  • HyperLogLog — cardinality estimation, approx_count_distinct, Redis PFCOUNT
  • Count-Min Sketch — frequency estimation, top-K items
  • Skip list — ordered, probabilistic, O(log n), Redis ZSET
  • Merkle tree — hash tree, integrity check, blockchain, Cassandra repair
  • Consistent hashing ring — MD5/SHA-1, virtual nodes, Amazon Dynamo

11. System Design — Component Design

  • URL Shortener — hash function, base62, collision handling, redirect, analytics
  • Rate Limiter — token bucket, sliding window, Redis INCR+EXPIRE, distributed
  • Key-Value Store — partition, replication, quorum, consistent hashing, versioning
  • Unique ID Generator — Snowflake ID, UUID v4/v7, ULID, Twitter Snowflake
  • Snowflake ID — 41-bit timestamp + 10-bit machineId + 12-bit sequence
  • Distributed Cache — Redis Cluster, Memcached, eviction policies (LRU, LFU, TTL)
  • Search System — index building, inverted index, ranking, Elasticsearch
  • Notification System — fan-out, push/pull, delivery guarantees, idempotency
  • News Feed / Timeline — fan-out-on-write vs fan-out-on-read, hybrid
  • Chat System — WebSocket, message ordering, delivery receipts, presence
  • Video Streaming — chunked upload, HLS/DASH, CDN, transcoding, metadata
  • Ride Sharing — geospatial index, QuadTree, Geohash, matching, surge pricing
  • Proximity Service — R-tree, KD-tree, Geohash, radius search
  • Typeahead / Autocomplete — trie, prefix search, top-K, Aho-Corasick
  • Web Crawler — BFS, politeness, deduplication, robots.txt, URL frontier
  • Google Drive / Dropbox — chunking, delta sync, conflict resolution, metadata DB
  • Payment System — idempotency, 2PC, saga, reconciliation, ledger
  • Hotel/Flight Booking — optimistic locking, inventory management, overbooking
  • Ad Click Aggregation — lambda architecture, deduplication, real-time + batch
  • Metrics / Monitoring — time-series DB, rollups, InfluxDB, Prometheus, alerting
  • Distributed Message Queue — replication, ordering, at-least-once, replay
  • Task Scheduler — distributed cron, ZooKeeper leader election, missed execution
  • API Rate Limiting — per-user, per-IP, sliding window, Redis INCR, headers
  • Authentication System — JWT, OAuth2, OIDC, session, token rotation

12. Microservices Patterns — Decomposition

  • Decompose by Business Capability — org structure alignment, Conway's Law
  • Decompose by Subdomain (DDD) — bounded context → service boundary
  • Strangler Fig — incremental migration, proxy, feature-by-feature replacement
  • Anti-Corruption Layer (ACL) — adapter between old and new system
  • Parallel Run — run old + new simultaneously, compare results
  • Branch by Abstraction — abstraction layer, swap implementation behind it
  • Monolith First — start monolith, extract services as boundaries clarify
  • Modular Monolith — well-defined modules, can extract to services later
  • Service granularity — too fine (chatty) vs too coarse (monolith), team cognitive load
  • Two-Pizza Rule — service team fits in a room, autonomous ownership
  • Seam — natural boundary in codebase, safe extraction point

13. Microservices Patterns — Communication

  • Synchronous — REST, gRPC, GraphQL, request-response, tight coupling
  • Asynchronous — message queues, event streaming, loose coupling
  • API Gateway — single entry point, routing, auth, rate limiting, aggregation
  • Backend for Frontend (BFF) — per-client API gateway, mobile vs web
  • Service-to-Service — direct HTTP, service mesh, mTLS
  • gRPC — strong typing, proto, streaming, bidirectional, code generation
  • REST API versioning — URI (/v1), header (Accept-version), media type
  • Consumer-Driven Contract (CDC) — Pact, consumer defines contract, provider verified
  • Service Mesh — Istio, Linkerd, Envoy, sidecar, transparent proxy
  • Circuit Breaker — Hystrix (deprecated), Resilience4j, CLOSED/OPEN/HALF-OPEN
  • Retry pattern — idempotent operations only, exponential backoff, jitter
  • Timeout pattern — fail fast, propagate deadline, gRPC deadline
  • Bulkhead pattern — thread pool per service, semaphore, isolate failures
  • Fallback — default response on failure, cache, static data
  • Request Hedging — send duplicate requests, take first response, reduce tail latency
  • Correlation ID — trace request across services, X-Correlation-ID header
  • Idempotency Key — X-Idempotency-Key, safe retry, at-least-once delivery
  • Health Check API — /health, /ready, liveness vs readiness vs startup
  • Service Registry — Eureka, Consul, etcd, Kubernetes DNS
  • Client-Side Discovery — client queries registry, load balances, Ribbon
  • Server-Side Discovery — load balancer queries registry, ALB, Nginx
  • Self Registration — service registers/deregisters on start/stop
  • Third-Party Registration — external registrar (Registrar), Kubernetes controller
  • Chained Requests — A → B → C, brittle, latency multiplication
  • Fan-out / Scatter-Gather — parallel calls, aggregate, timeout handling

14. Microservices Patterns — Data Management

  • Database per Service — autonomous data ownership, no shared schema
  • Shared Database (anti-pattern) — tight coupling, avoid in microservices
  • Saga Pattern — distributed transaction, choreography vs orchestration
  • Saga choreography — event chain, services react to events, no central coordinator
  • Saga orchestration — SagaOrchestrator, commands to services, handles compensation
  • Compensating Transaction — undo prior steps on failure, cancel, reverse
  • Outbox Pattern — DB + message in same transaction, CDC publishes events
  • Event Sourcing — state as events, aggregate, EventStore, projections
  • CQRS — command model (write), query model (read), eventual consistency
  • API Composition — query multiple services, aggregate in gateway/service
  • CQRS with Event Sourcing — commands → domain events → read-model projections
  • Transactional Outbox — outbox table, Message Relay, exactly-once publishing
  • Polling Publisher — poll outbox table, publish, mark processed
  • Transaction Log Tailing — Debezium, binlog/WAL, CDC → message broker
  • Domain Event — signals state change, contract, schema registry
  • Event-Carried State Transfer — embed enough state in event, avoid callbacks
  • Aggregate per Service — one microservice owns one aggregate
  • Anti-Entropy — background sync, reconcile state, Cassandra repair
  • Idempotent Consumer — deduplication store, messageId, processed check
  • Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) — failed messages, retry queue, human review

15. Microservices Patterns — Deployment & Operations

  • Single Service per Container — isolation, independent scaling, Docker
  • Serverless — FaaS, Lambda, Azure Functions, pay-per-invocation, cold start
  • Service Instance per VM/Container — blast radius, resource isolation
  • Multiple Services per Host — legacy, complex deployment, not recommended
  • Blue-Green Deployment — two identical environments, zero-downtime cutover
  • Canary Deployment — small traffic slice, metric-gated, progressive delivery
  • Shadow Deployment — mirror traffic, compare responses, no user impact
  • Feature Flags — LaunchDarkly, Unleash, FF4J, dark launch
  • Externalized Configuration — ConfigMap, Secrets, Spring Cloud Config, Vault
  • Log Aggregation — ELK, Loki, Fluent Bit, traceId, structured JSON
  • Distributed Tracing — OpenTelemetry, Jaeger, Zipkin, W3C traceparent
  • Health Check — liveness (is alive?), readiness (can serve?), startup (finished init?)
  • Circuit Breaker Monitoring — metrics, OPEN state alerting
  • Service Mesh Observability — Envoy metrics, Istio telemetry, mTLS
  • Sidecar Pattern — helper container, logging, proxy, config sync
  • Ambassador Pattern — proxy sidecar, offload cross-cutting concerns
  • Adapter Pattern (sidecar) — normalize heterogeneous services
  • GitOps — ArgoCD, Flux, declarative, Git as source of truth
  • Immutable Infrastructure — never modify running servers, replace entirely
  • Consumer-Driven Contract Testing — Pact, prevent breaking changes

16. Microservices Patterns — Security

  • API Gateway Authentication — JWT validation, OAuth2 Bearer token
  • Service-to-Service Auth — mTLS, SPIFFE/SPIRE, workload identity
  • Zero Trust — never trust always verify, microsegmentation, identity-centric
  • Token Propagation — forward JWT to downstream, Authorization header
  • Secrets Management — Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, SecretProviderClass
  • RBAC — role-based, ClusterRole, RoleBinding, attribute-based (ABAC)
  • OAuth2 flows — authorization code + PKCE (user), client credentials (service-to-service)
  • OIDC — identity layer on OAuth2, id_token, userinfo endpoint
  • Rate Limiting — API Gateway, Bucket4j, token bucket, per-client quotas
  • Audit Logging — who did what when, immutable audit trail, CloudTrail
  • Defense in Depth — network, application, data layers, multiple controls

17. Cloud Patterns

  • Auto Scaling — horizontal scale on metric (CPU, custom), cooldown, predictive
  • Elasticity — scale out under load, scale in when idle, stateless design required
  • Multi-Tenancy — silo (per-tenant infra), pool (shared), hybrid, tenant isolation
  • Tenant Isolation — row-level, schema-per-tenant, account-per-tenant
  • Serverless Pattern — event-driven, FaaS, BaaS, no server management
  • Managed Services — RDS, S3, SQS, offload undifferentiated work
  • Lift and Shift — rehost, minimal change, quick migration
  • Re-platforming — minor cloud optimizations, managed DB, container
  • Re-architecting — cloud-native, microservices, event-driven
  • Valet Key — pre-signed URL, time-limited, client accesses resource directly
  • Valet Key — S3 presigned URL, SAS token, direct upload/download
  • Federated Identity — SSO, external identity provider, SAML, OIDC, trust federation
  • Static Content Hosting — S3 + CloudFront, static files offload from app servers
  • Content Delivery Network — edge caches, Cache-Control, origin shield, purge
  • Queue-Based Load Leveling — SQS/Azure Queue buffer, absorb traffic spikes
  • Competing Consumers — multiple workers on same queue, parallel processing
  • Priority Queue — separate queues per priority, weighted consumers
  • Scheduler Agent Supervisor — distributed workflow, compensating actions
  • Leader Election — ZooKeeper, etcd, LeaderElection K8s, only one active worker
  • Compute Resource Consolidation — bin-pack workloads, reduce idle time
  • Retry Pattern — transient failures, exponential backoff, max attempts
  • Circuit Breaker (cloud) — prevent cascading, OPEN after threshold, self-heal
  • Health Endpoint Monitoring — GET /health, synthetic transactions, probes
  • Cache-Aside (Lazy Loading) — check cache → if miss, load from DB, write to cache
  • Write-Through Cache — write to cache + DB simultaneously, consistent
  • Write-Behind Cache — write to cache, async flush to DB, risk of loss
  • Read-Through Cache — cache loads from DB on miss transparently
  • Refresh-Ahead — proactively refresh before expiry, stale-while-revalidate
  • Sharding Pattern (cloud) — horizontal partition, shard key design, rebalancing
  • Index Table — secondary index on frequently queried non-key fields
  • Materialized View — pre-computed query result, refresh strategy
  • CQRS (cloud) — event-driven, separate read/write data stores, sync via events
  • Event Sourcing (cloud) — immutable event log, EventStore, EventBridge
  • Geodes — deploy replicas globally, route to nearest, reduce latency
  • Deployment Stamp — replicate stack per region/tenant, independent scaling
  • Bulkhead (cloud) — separate resource pools, limit blast radius, K8s namespace quotas
  • Throttling — cap resource usage, 429 Too Many Requests, Retry-After
  • Gateway Aggregation — combine multiple service responses in gateway
  • Gateway Offloading — move cross-cutting concerns to gateway (auth, SSL, logging)
  • Gateway Routing — route by path/header/host to different backends
  • Backends for Frontends (BFF) — per-client optimized gateway
  • Sidecar — auxiliary container, co-located, same lifecycle
  • Ambassador — proxy sidecar, handle outbound traffic
  • Anti-Corruption Layer (cloud) — translate between legacy and cloud-native models
  • Strangler Fig (cloud) — incrementally replace legacy with cloud-native
  • External Configuration Store — ConfigMap, Vault, Parameter Store, not in code
  • Asynchronous Request-Reply — 202 Accepted, polling URL, Location header
  • Claim Check — offload payload to blob, pass reference in message
  • Choreography — services react to events, no central coordinator
  • Saga (cloud) — long-running transaction, compensation, Saga log
  • Transactional Outbox — DB + message atomically, CDC publishes
  • Compensating Transaction — undo previously committed work, business-level
  • Sequential Convoy — process messages in order per correlation key
  • Pipes and Filters (cloud) — composable processing stages, Lambda chain

18. Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA) Patterns

  • SOA Fundamentals — loose coupling, service contract, abstraction, reusability, autonomy, statelessness, discoverability, composability
  • Service Contract — WSDL, SOAP, XML Schema, interface definition
  • Enterprise Service Bus (ESB) — central bus, message routing, transformation, orchestration
  • ESB — MuleESB, IBM MQ, TIBCO, WSO2, Apache Camel
  • Service Registry / Repository — UDDI, service catalog, metadata, governance
  • UDDI — Universal Description, Discovery, and Integration
  • SOAP — envelope, header, body, WS-* standards
  • WS-Security — SAML token, X.509 certificate, UsernameToken
  • WS-ReliableMessaging — at-least-once, exactly-once delivery guarantees
  • WS-AtomicTransaction — 2PC over web services, Coordinator, Participant
  • WS-BusinessActivity — long-running, compensation-based transactions
  • BPEL (Business Process Execution Language) — orchestration workflow, XML-based
  • Orchestration vs Choreography — central controller vs event-driven cooperation
  • Service Aggregator — compose multiple services into one
  • Service Façade — simplify complex service interactions
  • Service Decomposition — functional, entity, utility, task services
  • Service Layering — task services → entity services → utility services
  • Canonical Data Model — common data format, avoid point-to-point transformations
  • Message Translator — transform message format between services
  • Message Router — content-based routing, correlation-based routing
  • Message Filter — discard irrelevant messages
  • Splitter — split composite message into parts
  • Aggregator — combine correlated messages into one
  • Scatter-Gather — broadcast + aggregate responses
  • Process Manager — maintain state of multi-step process, correlate messages
  • Correlation Identifier — match request with reply, CorrelationId header
  • Return Address — ReplyTo header, where to send response
  • Dead Letter Channel — undeliverable messages, inspection queue
  • Message Expiration — TTL on messages, discard stale
  • Datatype Channel — typed channel, only one type per channel
  • Idempotent Receiver — safe message redelivery, deduplication
  • Transactional Client — enlist message operations in transaction
  • Service Virtualization — mock service for testing, WireMock, Mountebank
  • Policy Injection — cross-cutting policies attached to services (AOP-like)
  • Intermediary — transformer, validator, router between services
  • Data Enrichment — augment message with data from other sources
  • Normalizer — multiple message formats → single canonical format
  • Content Enricher — add missing data by querying external source
  • Envelope Wrapper — add metadata wrapper to message payload
  • Command Message — message instructing receiver to perform action
  • Event Message — notification of state change
  • Document Message — data transfer, no action instruction
  • Request-Reply — synchronous over async, CorrelationId + ReplyTo
  • Publish-Subscribe Channel — broadcast to all subscribers
  • Point-to-Point Channel — exactly one receiver
  • Competing Consumers — multiple receivers on same channel
  • Message Broker — decouple producers/consumers, routing, transformation
  • Claim Check (SOA) — store large payload externally, pass reference
  • Smart Proxy — log interactions, introduce behavior transparently

19. Big Data Patterns

  • Lambda Architecture — batch layer + speed layer + serving layer
  • Lambda — batch: high latency, accurate; speed: low latency, approximate; serving: merge
  • Lambda — batch views (Spark), real-time views (Flink/Storm), Apache Druid serving
  • Lambda pitfalls — code duplication (batch + stream), operational complexity
  • Kappa Architecture — stream only, reprocess by replaying from log
  • Kappa — Kafka as source of truth, one processing layer, simpler than Lambda
  • Kappa vs Lambda — complexity vs correctness trade-off
  • Medallion Architecture — Bronze (raw), Silver (cleansed), Gold (aggregated)
  • Bronze → raw ingestion, idempotent writes, preserve all data
  • Silver → validated, deduplicated, enriched, business-ready
  • Gold → aggregated, domain-specific, BI/ML ready
  • Data Lakehouse — Delta Lake / Iceberg / Hudi, ACID on object storage
  • Lakehouse — eliminates data lake + data warehouse dual architecture
  • Data Lake — raw storage, schema-on-read, cheap, multi-format
  • Data Warehouse — structured, schema-on-write, OLAP, columnar, MPP
  • Data Mart — subject-specific subset of warehouse, departmental
  • OLAP vs OLTP — analytical vs transactional, columnar vs row, aggregation vs CRUD
  • Star Schema — fact table + dimension tables, denormalized, fast aggregation
  • Star — FactSales, DimCustomer, DimProduct, DimDate
  • Snowflake Schema — normalized dimensions, sub-dimensions, less redundancy
  • Galaxy Schema — multiple fact tables sharing dimensions, complex
  • Slowly Changing Dimension (SCD) — Type 0 (retain), Type 1 (overwrite), Type 2 (history), Type 3 (prev+curr)
  • SCD Type 2 — effective_date, expiry_date, is_current, surrogate key
  • Fact table types — transaction, periodic snapshot, accumulating snapshot
  • Inmon vs Kimball — normalized DW (EDW → marts) vs dimensional (marts first)
  • ETL vs ELT — transform before load (ETL) vs load then transform (ELT)
  • Data Ingestion Patterns — batch, micro-batch, streaming, CDC
  • CDC (Change Data Capture) — Debezium, binlog, WAL, log-based vs query-based
  • Incremental Load — high-water mark, watermark column, last_modified
  • Full Load — truncate + reload, simple, expensive
  • Upsert (MERGE) — MERGE INTO, insert or update, idempotent
  • Idempotent Ingestion — rerunnable pipelines, no duplicates on retry
  • Exactly-Once Ingestion — transactional writes, offset tracking
  • Partitioning — date, region, category — partition pruning performance
  • Bucketing — hash on join/group key, eliminate shuffle
  • Compaction — merge small files, OPTIMIZE, reduce metadata overhead
  • ZORDER / Data Clustering — multi-dim, data skipping, Liquid Clustering
  • Data Skipping — min/max per file, bloom filters, column stats
  • Partition Pruning — skip partitions not matching filter
  • Predicate Pushdown — filter at storage layer, reduce I/O
  • Projection Pushdown — read only required columns
  • Columnar Storage — Parquet, ORC, Arrow — column access, compression
  • Row Group / Stripe — Parquet / ORC physical grouping, statistics
  • Vectorized Processing — SIMD, batch column operations, Arrow
  • Stream Processing Patterns — stateless, stateful, windowing
  • Windowing — tumbling, hopping, sliding, session, global
  • Watermarking — late data handling, event time vs processing time
  • Exactly-Once Streaming — checkpoint + idempotent sink
  • Micro-batch vs Continuous — Spark Structured Streaming vs Flink continuous
  • Event Time vs Processing Time — when event occurred vs when processed
  • Out-of-Order Events — buffering, grace period, late data policy
  • Complex Event Processing (CEP) — pattern matching over event streams, Flink CEP
  • Backfill Pattern — historical data processing, bounded replay
  • Reprocessing Pattern — replay from source, idempotent consumers
  • Fanout Pattern — one source, multiple consumers, independent processing
  • Data Quality Pattern — Great Expectations, Deequ, dbt tests, assertion framework
  • Schema Registry Pattern — centralized schema, compatibility enforcement
  • Data Lineage — Apache Atlas, Purview, OpenLineage, Marquez
  • Data Catalog — AWS Glue, Apache Hive Metastore, Unity Catalog, Datahub
  • Data Mesh — domain ownership, data as product, self-serve platform, federated governance
  • Data Mesh — four principles: domain ownership, data as product, self-serve infra, federated governance
  • Data Product — discoverable, addressable, trustworthy, self-describing, interoperable
  • Data Contract — schema, SLO, ownership, lineage — agreed between producer/consumer
  • Feature Store — offline (batch), online (low-latency), point-in-time correct joins
  • Feature Store — Feast, Tecton, Databricks Feature Store, Hopsworks
  • Replay Architecture — immutable log as source of truth, replay to rebuild state
  • Time Travel — query historical snapshots, Delta VERSION AS OF, Iceberg AS OF TIMESTAMP
  • Hot/Warm/Cold Tiering — storage tier by access frequency, cost optimization
  • Tiered Storage — Kafka tiered storage, Iceberg remote storage, S3 Glacier
  • Shuffle-Heavy vs Shuffle-Light — design pipelines to minimize shuffle
  • Broadcast Join Pattern — small lookup table broadcast, avoid shuffle join
  • Skew Handling — salt key, AQE skew join, separate pass for hot keys
  • Denormalization — pre-join, reduce query complexity, write vs read trade-off
  • Materialized Aggregations — pre-computed totals, REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW
  • Serving Layer Patterns — OLAP cube, columnar DB, Druid, Pinot, ClickHouse
  • Lambda + Kappa Hybrid — batch for accuracy, stream for freshness, merge in serving

20. Data Patterns — Storage & Access

  • Repository Pattern — domain-level collection abstraction, findById, save
  • DAO (Data Access Object) — DB-level CRUD, UserDao, getById(), insert()
  • Active Record — object maps to DB row, contains persistence logic (Rails, Django)
  • Data Mapper — separate mapper class, objects unaware of persistence (Hibernate)
  • Identity Map — cache loaded objects, prevent duplicate DB loads, unit-of-work
  • Unit of Work — track changes, single commit, Session, DbContext, EntityManager
  • Lazy Loading — load related data on access, proxy, virtual proxy, N+1 risk
  • Eager Loading — join fetch, @EntityGraph, prevent N+1, memory trade-off
  • Explicit Loading — manual load() call, selective, entry.Reference().Load()
  • Query Object — encapsulate query as object, composable, type-safe
  • Specification Pattern — isSatisfiedBy(), composable predicates, repository integration
  • CQRS (data level) — separate read/write models, read-optimized denormalized views
  • Event Store — append-only, EventId, AggregateId, Version, EventType, Payload
  • Snapshot Store — aggregate state snapshot, skip event replay, SnapshotThreshold
  • Projection — rebuild read model from events, idempotent, versioned
  • Read Model — denormalized, query-optimized, eventual consistency
  • Write Model — aggregate, validates invariants, emits events
  • Change Data Capture — listen to DB log, publish events, Debezium, Maxwell
  • Inbox Pattern — store incoming messages in DB before processing, idempotent
  • Outbox Pattern — store outgoing messages in DB, CDC/polling publishes
  • Transactional Outbox — same DB transaction, no dual-write problem
  • Saga State Machine — persist saga state, resume after crash, SagaRepository
  • Temporal Table — system-versioned, PERIOD FOR SYSTEM_TIME, history queries
  • Bi-Temporal Data — valid time + transaction time, historical accuracy
  • Soft Delete — deleted_at flag, is_deleted, exclude from queries, GDPR compliance
  • Optimistic Offline Lock — version column, detect conflicts across long transactions
  • Pessimistic Offline Lock — explicit lock record, LockManager, prevent simultaneous edits
  • Coarse-Grained Lock — lock aggregate, not individual items
  • Implicit Lock — framework manages lock (Hibernate @Version)
  • Multi-Table Inheritance — one table per class hierarchy level, JPA @Joined
  • Single Table Inheritance — all subclasses in one table, dtype discriminator
  • Concrete Table Inheritance — one table per concrete class, no joins
  • Class Table Inheritance — one table per class, joins on read
  • Polymorphic Associations — entity_type + entity_id, flexible FK
  • EAV (Entity-Attribute-Value) — flexible schema, anti-pattern for querying
  • JSON / Document Store — schema-flexible, JSONB, MongoDB, query on JSON fields
  • Wide Column — Cassandra, HBase, column families, sparse rows
  • Time-Series Patterns — InfluxDB, Timescale, downsampling, continuous aggregates
  • Graph Patterns — adjacency list, nested set, materialized path, closure table
  • Closure table — tree_path table, all ancestor-descendant pairs, fast tree queries
  • Full-Text Index — GIN, tsvector, inverted index, pg_trgm
  • Composite Index — multi-column, column order matters, selectivity
  • Partial Index — condition on index, WHERE active = true
  • Covering Index — include all queried columns, index-only scan
  • Index Intersection — combine two indexes, optimizer decision
  • Write Amplification — LSM tree compaction, index maintenance overhead
  • Connection Pooling Pattern — PgBouncer, HikariCP, pool sizing formula
  • Database Fan-Out — write to multiple DBs, async replication
  • Read Replica Routing — route reads to replica, writes to primary
  • Multi-Master Replication — Galera, CockroachDB, conflict resolution
  • Schema Migration Pattern — Flyway, Liquibase, Alembic, versioned, repeatable
  • Expand-Contract (Parallel Change) — add new, migrate, remove old, zero-downtime
  • Blue-Green DB Migration — dual-write, backfill, switch read, remove old
  • Shadow Table — dual-write to old + new, compare, cutover
  • Event-Driven Data Sync — publish events on change, consumers update own models
  • Data Vault — Hub, Link, Satellite, historized, audit-friendly, scalable DW

21. Distributed Systems Patterns

  • Two Generals Problem — impossibility of coordinated agreement over unreliable network
  • Byzantine Fault Tolerance — handle malicious/arbitrary failures, PBFT, f < n/3
  • FLP Impossibility — no deterministic consensus in async system with one faulty process
  • Paxos — Proposer, Acceptor, Learner, prepare/promise/accept/accepted
  • Raft — leader election, log replication, term, RequestVote, AppendEntries
  • Multi-Paxos — single leader, stream of consensus, fewer round trips
  • View Stamped Replication — alternative to Paxos, view change protocol
  • Zab (ZooKeeper Atomic Broadcast) — leader election + broadcast, epoch
  • Epoch — monotonic term counter, fencing, leader validity
  • Fencing Token — monotonic ID, prevent stale leader writes, IF version > stored
  • Single Leader Replication — one writer, followers replicate, failover
  • Multi-Leader Replication — multiple writers, conflict resolution, last-write-wins
  • Leaderless Replication — Dynamo-style, quorum reads/writes, sloppy quorum
  • Sloppy Quorum — accept writes even if quorum nodes unavailable, hinted handoff
  • Hinted Handoff — store writes for unavailable nodes, deliver when recovered
  • Read Repair — detect + fix stale reads during read, Cassandra
  • Anti-Entropy — background sync, Merkle tree comparison, repair
  • Version Vectors — track causality per replica, detect concurrent updates
  • CRDT (Conflict-free Replicated Data Types) — G-Counter, PN-Counter, OR-Set, LWW-Register
  • Linearizability — most recent value, operations appear instantaneous, strongest consistency
  • Serializability — transactions execute as if serial, isolation level
  • Causal Consistency — causally related ops in order, concurrent ops in any order
  • Eventual Consistency — all replicas converge given no new updates
  • Monotonic Read Consistency — never see older data after reading newer
  • Read Your Writes — after write, always see that write in reads
  • Session Consistency — consistency within a session (client context)
  • Bounded Staleness — read at most K versions old, or within T time
  • Strong Eventual Consistency — CRDT-based, identical state if same updates applied
  • Logical Clock — Lamport timestamp, max(local, received) + 1
  • Hybrid Logical Clock (HLC) — physical + logical, {wall, logical}, causality + ordering
  • Distributed Snapshot — Chandy-Lamport algorithm, consistent global state
  • Failure Detector — phi accrual, heartbeat-based, SWIM protocol
  • Gossip Protocol — infection-style dissemination, eventual convergence, membership
  • Two-Phase Locking (2PL) — growing + shrinking phases, no deadlock + no lost update
  • MVCC — snapshot isolation, read consistent view, no read locks
  • Optimistic Concurrency Control (OCC) — validate on commit, retry on conflict
  • Distributed Lock — Redlock (Redis), Chubby, ZooKeeper ephemeral node
  • Lease — time-limited lock, auto-expire, prevents indefinite hold
  • Distributed Counter — CRDT G-Counter, Riak, Redis INCR
  • Consistent Hash Ring — virtual nodes, token ranges, Cassandra, Dynamo
  • Write Quorum — W > N/2 for strong write consistency
  • Read Quorum — R > N/2, W + R > N for linearizability
  • Chain Replication — head writes, tail reads, strong consistency, high throughput
  • Primary-Backup — primary handles ops, backup replicates, failover
  • Log-Structured Storage — WAL, LSM, immutable append, Kafka, RocksDB
  • Shared Nothing Architecture — no shared state, scale horizontally, Snowflake
  • Shared Disk Architecture — shared storage, different compute, Aurora
  • Shared Memory Architecture — multi-CPU, UMA/NUMA, vertical scaling

22. Integration Patterns (Enterprise)

  • Message Channel — point-to-point, publish-subscribe, dead letter
  • Message — header + body, MessageId, CorrelationId, Timestamp, ContentType
  • Pipes and Filters — processing pipeline, composable Filter components
  • Message Router — content-based, correlation-based, dynamic routing
  • Message Translator — format transformation, canonical model mapping
  • Message Endpoint — sender, receiver, polling consumer, event-driven consumer
  • Polling Consumer — actively polls queue, rate control
  • Event-Driven Consumer — push-based, callback on arrival
  • Competing Consumers — multiple workers, parallel processing, maxConcurrency
  • Message Dispatcher — selective consumer, exclusive consumer
  • Selective Consumer — filter on message properties, messageSelector
  • Durable Subscriber — offline subscriber, messages queued until reconnect
  • Idempotent Receiver — process message once, messageId dedup store
  • Transactional Client — message + DB in one transaction
  • Request-Reply — sync over async, ReplyTo, CorrelationId, timeout
  • Return Address — JMSReplyTo, reply_to property
  • Correlation Identifier — link request to reply, UUID
  • Message Sequence — split + reassemble, SequenceId, SequenceSize
  • Message Expiration — TimeToLive, discard stale
  • Dead Letter Channel — unprocessable messages, DLQ, x-dead-letter-exchange
  • Guaranteed Delivery — durable queue, persisted messages, ack
  • Channel Purger — remove all messages, testing cleanup
  • Wire Tap — copy to secondary channel without disrupting flow, logging
  • Message History — track channel hops, audit, X-Message-History
  • Control Bus — manage messaging system via control messages
  • Detour — temporarily re-route through validation/audit component
  • Smart Proxy — transparent recording/auditing proxy
  • Test Message — synthetic message, verify channel health
  • Envelope Wrapper — wrap payload with metadata envelope
  • Content Filter — remove unnecessary data from message
  • Claim Check — store large payload externally, pass reference token
  • Normalizer — route multiple formats through translators to canonical form
  • Canonical Data Model — common enterprise data model, reduce N×M to N+M transformations
  • Aggregator — correlate and combine related messages
  • Resequencer — reorder out-of-sequence messages
  • Composed Message Processor — split → process → aggregate
  • Scatter-Gather — broadcast + collect responses
  • Process Manager — stateful multi-step process coordinator
  • Routing Slip — list of processors attached to message, self-routing
  • Publish-Subscribe Channel — topic, all subscribers receive copy
  • Datatype Channel — one channel per message type, type safety
  • Invalid Message Channel — unprocessable format, separate channel

23. API Design Patterns

  • REST — resources, HTTP verbs, stateless, HATEOAS, Richardson Maturity Model
  • Level 0 — single URI, POST everything
  • Level 1 — resource URIs
  • Level 2 — HTTP verbs, status codes
  • Level 3 — HATEOAS, hypermedia controls
  • REST best practices — noun URIs, plural, versioning, ETag, If-None-Match
  • HTTP status codes — 200/201/204, 400/401/403/404/409/422, 500/503
  • Idempotent HTTP methods — GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD — safe retry
  • Non-idempotent — POST (create), PATCH (partial update) — idempotency key needed
  • REST versioning — URI path (/v1), query param, header (Accept-version), media type
  • Pagination — offset+limit, cursor (after), keyset, Link header, X-Total-Count
  • Filtering — ?status=active, ?created_after=2024, OData $filter
  • Sorting — ?sort=created_at:desc, multiple fields
  • GraphQL — single endpoint, client-specified shape, Query, Mutation, Subscription
  • GraphQL — DataLoader for N+1, @defer, @stream, fragments, introspection
  • GraphQL — schema-first vs code-first, SDL, resolvers, context
  • gRPC — proto3, unary, server streaming, client streaming, bidirectional streaming
  • gRPC — Deadline, Metadata, Interceptor, reflection, health check proto
  • OpenAPI / Swagger — spec-first, code generation, $ref, oneOf/anyOf/allOf
  • AsyncAPI — event-driven API spec, channels, messages, bindings
  • API Gateway Pattern — routing, auth, rate limit, transform, cache, circuit break
  • BFF (Backend for Frontend) — per-client API, aggregation, tailored response
  • API Composition — aggregate multiple service responses
  • Strangler Fig API — route old calls to new implementation, incremental migration
  • Facade API — simplify complex service interactions behind single API
  • Tolerant Reader — ignore unknown fields, robust to schema evolution
  • Consumer-Driven Contracts — Pact, provider verifies consumer expectations
  • Hypermedia (HATEOAS) — _links, rel, href, discoverability
  • API Versioning Strategies — additive changes safe, breaking changes require version
  • Backward Compatibility — add optional fields, never remove/rename required
  • Forward Compatibility — consumers ignore unknown fields (tolerant reader)
  • API Rate Limiting — X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, Retry-After
  • API Key — X-API-Key, rotation, per-client, least privilege
  • OAuth2 — Authorization Code, Client Credentials, PKCE, Refresh Token
  • JWT — header.payload.signature, exp, iat, sub, RS256 vs HS256
  • Webhook — outbound HTTP push, event notification, HMAC signature verification
  • Long Polling — hold connection, respond on event, fallback for WebSocket
  • SSE (Server-Sent Events) — text/event-stream, one-directional push, auto-reconnect
  • WebSocket — Upgrade: websocket, full-duplex, ping/pong, close frame
  • Circuit Breaker API — 503 Service Unavailable, Retry-After header
  • API Observability — X-Request-ID, distributed trace, access log, metrics

24. Resilience & Reliability Patterns

  • Redundancy — eliminate SPOF, N+1, active-active, active-passive
  • Replication — data redundancy, leader-follower, multi-leader, leaderless
  • Geographic Distribution — multi-region, multi-AZ, latency vs consistency
  • Failover — automatic switchover on failure, DNS failover, floating IP
  • Health Check — liveness, readiness, startup, deep health vs shallow
  • Circuit Breaker — half-open probe, failure rate, slow call rate threshold
  • Retry — idempotent only, bounded retries, exponential backoff + jitter
  • Timeout — connect timeout, read timeout, deadline propagation
  • Fallback — cached response, default value, degraded response
  • Bulkhead — thread pool isolation, connection pool per dependency
  • Backpressure — bounded queue, pause(), reactive streams request(n)
  • Load Shedding — drop requests under overload, priority-based shedding
  • Graceful Degradation — partial service, disable non-essential features
  • Chaos Engineering — Netflix Chaos Monkey, Gremlin, GameDay
  • Disaster Recovery — RTO (recovery time objective), RPO (recovery point objective)
  • DR tiers — Backup/Restore (hours), Pilot Light (minutes), Warm Standby (seconds), Multi-Site (instant)
  • Immutable Infrastructure — replace don't patch, predictable deployments
  • Idempotency — safe retries, POST with idempotency key
  • Exactly-Once Processing — idempotent consumer + exactly-once producer
  • Compensating Transaction — business-level undo, Saga compensation
  • Checkpointing — periodic state snapshot, resume from last checkpoint
  • Heartbeat — liveness signal, failure detection threshold
  • Gossip-Based Failure Detection — SWIM, phi accrual, probabilistic
  • Split-Brain Prevention — quorum, fencing token, STONITH
  • Rolling Upgrade — one instance at a time, no downtime, compatibility required
  • Blue-Green — instant cutover, immediate rollback, full double capacity
  • Canary — small slice, metric-gated, Flagger, automatic promotion/rollback
  • Feature Toggle — runtime on/off, A/B test, dark launch, kill switch
  • Self-Healing — Kubernetes restart policy, auto-remediation, EventBridge → SSM
  • Error Budget — SLO allowance, burn rate, freeze deployments on exhaust

25. Staff / Principal Engineering Competencies

  • Architecture Decision Records (ADR) — structured decisions, context, options, decision, consequences
  • ADR formats — MADR, Nygard, RFC-style, lightweight
  • Technology Radar — adopt, trial, assess, hold — ThoughtWorks format
  • Technical Debt Quadrant — reckless/prudent × deliberate/inadvertent (Fowler)
  • Technical debt classification — code debt, architectural debt, test debt, documentation debt
  • Fitness Functions — automated architecture validation, ArchUnit, ArchTest
  • Evolutionary Architecture — incremental change, guided by fitness functions
  • Conway's Law — systems mirror communication structures of organizations
  • Inverse Conway Maneuver — restructure teams to get desired architecture
  • Team Topologies — Stream-aligned, Platform, Enabling, Complicated Subsystem teams
  • Cognitive Load — team cognitive load limit, service size, module boundaries
  • Platform Engineering — internal developer platform, paved paths, self-service
  • Developer Experience (DX) — DORA metrics, deployment frequency, lead time, MTTR
  • DORA Metrics — Deployment Frequency, Lead Time for Changes, MTTR, Change Failure Rate
  • Four Key Metrics — engineering performance, elite vs low performers
  • System Thinking — feedback loops, emergent behavior, reinforcing vs balancing loops
  • Trade-off Analysis — consistency vs availability, latency vs throughput, cost vs reliability
  • Non-Functional Requirements (NFR) — performance, scalability, security, observability, maintainability
  • Architecture Patterns Selection — context matters, no silver bullet
  • RFC (Request for Comments) — proposal format, stakeholder review, async decision
  • Architecture Review Board (ARB) — governance, standards enforcement, exceptions
  • Threat Modeling — STRIDE, PASTA, attack tree, data flow diagram
  • STRIDE — Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, Elevation of Privilege
  • Secure by Design — threat model first, security requirements, OWASP Top 10
  • Zero Trust Architecture — never trust always verify, microsegmentation, identity-first
  • 12-Factor App — codebase, dependencies, config, backing services, build/release/run, processes, port binding, concurrency, disposability, dev/prod parity, logs, admin processes
  • 15-Factor App — extends 12-factor with API first, telemetry, auth/authz
  • Domain Storytelling — workshop technique, domain experts tell stories, extract model
  • Event Storming — collaborative modeling, domain events, commands, aggregates, bounded contexts
  • Example Mapping — BDD, rules + examples + questions, acceptance criteria
  • Architectural Kata — practice designing systems, constraints, requirements
  • Modifiability — ability to change, cohesion, coupling, encapsulation
  • Observability — metrics, logs, traces — the three pillars
  • OpenTelemetry — unified observability, OTLP, auto-instrumentation
  • Strangler Fig Strategy — incremental modernization, proxy, feature-by-feature
  • Microservices Migration — decompose by seam, extract bounded context, anti-corruption layer
  • Shared Nothing vs Shared Kernel — data ownership, shared code careful management
  • API-First Design — OpenAPI spec before implementation, mock-driven development
  • Contract-First Development — define contract (Avro/Protobuf/OpenAPI), generate code
  • Hiring & Calibration — system design rubrics, architectural thinking assessment
  • Mentorship — design review culture, architecture office hours, decision documentation
  • Engineering Principles — define explicitly, apply consistently, revisit regularly

26. System Design — Advanced Scenarios

  • Designing a globally distributed key-value store — consistent hashing, quorum, CRDT, vector clocks
  • Designing a distributed message queue — replication, ordering, compaction, consumer groups
  • Designing a real-time collaborative editor — OT vs CRDT, conflict resolution, presence
  • Designing a distributed rate limiter — token bucket, Redis Lua, sliding window, multi-region
  • Designing a multi-region active-active database — conflict resolution, causal consistency, CRDT
  • Designing a financial ledger system — double-entry, immutable, event sourcing, reconciliation
  • Designing a search engine — crawling, indexing, ranking, BM25, distributed index
  • Designing a social graph — adjacency list, distributed graph, Neo4j, friend-of-friend
  • Designing a recommendation engine — collaborative filtering, content-based, matrix factorization
  • Designing a real-time bidding system — sub-100ms, auction logic, budget pacing
  • Designing a ride-sharing matching system — geospatial, supply/demand, dynamic pricing
  • Designing a large-scale ML platform — feature store, training pipeline, serving, monitoring
  • Designing a multi-tenant SaaS platform — isolation tiers, tenant onboarding, billing
  • Designing a zero-downtime migration — expand-contract, dual-write, shadow table, cutover
  • Designing an event-driven microservices system — Kafka, Saga, outbox, idempotency, CDC
  • Designing a data mesh platform — domain ownership, data product contract, self-serve infra
  • Designing a distributed cache — consistent hashing, eviction, replication, hot key handling
  • Designing an observability platform — OTLP pipeline, Prometheus, Loki, Tempo, alerting
  • Designing a streaming data platform — Kafka → Flink → Iceberg → BI → real-time serving
  • Designing API versioning strategy — additive, sunset headers, contract testing, migration guide

Cloud - Azure · AWS · Snowflake · Databricks

Cloud Topics

1. Cloud Fundamentals & Architecture Principles

  • Cloud service models — IaaS, PaaS, SaaS, FaaS, CaaS
  • Deployment models — public, private, hybrid, multi-cloud
  • Shared responsibility model — AWS vs Azure variations
  • Well-Architected Framework — AWS (6 pillars) vs Azure (5 pillars)
  • Reliability pillar — fault tolerance, DR, RTO, RPO, MTTR, MTBF
  • Security pillar — defense in depth, zero trust, least privilege
  • Performance efficiency — right-sizing, elasticity, benchmarking
  • Cost optimization — reserved vs on-demand vs spot, rightsizing, tagging
  • Operational excellence — IaC, observability, runbooks, postmortems
  • Sustainability pillar — carbon footprint, efficient workloads
  • High availability — active-active, active-passive, multi-region, multi-AZ
  • Fault domains, update domains — AWS AZ vs Azure FD/UD
  • CAP theorem — partition tolerance, CP vs AP systems
  • Consistency models — strong, eventual, bounded staleness, session, monotonic
  • Cell-based architecture — blast radius reduction, independent cells
  • Bulkhead pattern — resource isolation, failure containment
  • Strangler fig pattern — incremental migration to cloud
  • Anti-patterns — lift-and-shift pitfalls, cloud sprawl, shadow IT
  • FinOps — unit economics, cost allocation, showback vs chargeback
  • Landing zone — account/subscription structure, guardrails, governance

2. AWS Core Compute

  • EC2 — instance families (C, M, R, G, P, Inf, Trn), generations
  • EC2 — On-Demand, Reserved (Standard/Convertible), Savings Plans, Spot
  • EC2 — Spot interruption handling, Spot Fleet, CapacityRebalancing
  • EC2 — placement groups (cluster, spread, partition), tenancy
  • EC2 — instance metadata service — IMDSv2, X-aws-ec2-metadata-token
  • EC2 — user data, cloud-init, AMI, golden AMI pipeline
  • EC2 — Nitro hypervisor, Nitro Enclaves, bare metal instances
  • EC2 — Graviton3 (Arm), x86 vs Arm performance/cost
  • Auto Scaling — ASG, launch template, scaling policies (target tracking, step, scheduled)
  • ASG — InstanceRefresh, warm pools, lifecycle hooks
  • ECS — task definitions, services, awsvpc networking, FARGATE vs EC2 launch type
  • ECS — service auto scaling, capacity providers, DAEMON scheduling
  • EKS — managed node groups, Fargate profiles, aws-node CNI, IRSA
  • EKS — cluster autoscaler, Karpenter, kube-proxy mode
  • Lambda — invocation models (sync, async, event source mapping)
  • Lambda — cold start, SnapStart (Java), provisioned concurrency
  • Lambda — execution environment, /tmp storage, Lambda layers
  • Lambda — ReservedConcurrency, UnreservedConcurrency, throttling
  • Lambda — extensions, LAMBDA_TASK_ROOT, AWS_LAMBDA_FUNCTION_VERSION
  • Lambda — EventSourceMapping — SQS, Kinesis, DynamoDB Streams, BisectOnError
  • Batch — job queues, compute environments, array jobs, SPOT strategy
  • Fargate — serverless containers, Fargate Spot, ephemeralStorage
  • App Runner — managed container service, auto scaling, VPC connector
  • Lightsail — simplified VMs, bundled pricing, blueprints

3. AWS Networking

  • VPC — CIDR design, subnets (public/private/isolated), AZ mapping
  • VPC — internet gateway, NAT gateway, egress-only IGW
  • VPC — route tables, local route, propagation, longest prefix match
  • Security Groups — stateful, inbound/outbound rules, 0.0.0.0/0 risks
  • NACLs — stateless, numbered rules, ephemeral ports
  • VPC Peering — transitive routing limitation, CIDR overlap restriction
  • Transit Gateway — hub-and-spoke, route tables, attachments, ECMP
  • PrivateLink — endpoint services, interface endpoints, DNS resolution
  • VPC Endpoints — gateway (S3, DynamoDB), interface, policy
  • Direct Connect — dedicated, hosted, LAG, virtual interfaces (public/private/transit)
  • Direct Connect — failover with VPN, BFD, BGP AS_PATH prepending
  • Site-to-Site VPN — CGW, VGW, IKEv2, DPD, dead peer detection
  • Route 53 — hosted zones, record types (A, AAAA, CNAME, Alias, MX, TXT)
  • Route 53 — routing policies (simple, weighted, latency, failover, geolocation, geoproximity, multivalue)
  • Route 53 — health checks, HTTPS health checks, calculated health checks
  • Route 53 — private hosted zones, resolver, forwarding rules, hybrid DNS
  • CloudFront — distributions, origins, behaviors, cache policies, TTL
  • CloudFront — signed URLs vs signed cookies, OAC (origin access control)
  • CloudFront — Lambda@Edge, CloudFront Functions, viewer-request/response
  • Global Accelerator — anycast, endpoint groups, traffic dials
  • Elastic Load Balancing — ALB, NLB, GWLB — use cases, differences
  • ALB — listener rules, target groups, weighted routing, sticky sessions
  • NLB — TCP/UDP, TLS termination, static IP, zonal isolation
  • WAF — managed rules, rate-based rules, web ACL, CAPTCHA
  • Shield — Standard (always-on) vs Advanced — DDoS protection

4. AWS Storage

  • S3 — storage classes (Standard, IA, One Zone-IA, Glacier Instant/Flexible/Deep Archive, Intelligent-Tiering)
  • S3 — lifecycle policies, Transition, Expiration, AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload
  • S3 — versioning, MFA delete, object lock (Governance, Compliance), WORM
  • S3 — replication (CRR, SRR), replication time control (RTC), ReplicaModifications
  • S3 — presigned URLs, PutObject, expiry, x-amz-security-token
  • S3 — multipart upload, TransferManager, parallel parts, s3:AbortMultipartUpload
  • S3 — S3 Select, Glacier Select, SQL expressions on objects
  • S3 — Event Notifications → SQS/SNS/Lambda/EventBridge
  • S3 — Access Points, Multi-Region Access Points, Object Lambda
  • S3 — encryption (SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, SSE-C, DSSE-KMS), aws:kms
  • S3 — bucket policies, ACLs (legacy), PublicAccessBlock, aws:PrincipalOrgID
  • EBS — volume types (gp3, io2 Block Express, st1, sc1), IOPS, throughput
  • EBS — io2 Block Express — 256K IOPS, 4K throughput, multi-attach
  • EBS — snapshots, incremental, EBS-Backed AMI, Fast Snapshot Restore
  • EBS — encryption, aws:kms, encrypted snapshot copy
  • EFS — General Purpose vs Max I/O, Bursting vs Provisioned throughput
  • EFS — access points, EFS Replication, lifecycle management, Intelligent-Tiering
  • FSx — FSx for Lustre (HPC), FSx for Windows File Server, FSx for NetApp ONTAP, FSx for OpenZFS
  • Storage Gateway — File, Volume, Tape gateways, hybrid cloud storage
  • Backup — backup plans, vault lock, cross-region/cross-account backup
  • DataSync — online data transfer, agent, task, filtering, bandwidth throttle

5. AWS Databases

  • RDS — supported engines (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, Oracle, SQL Server)
  • RDS — Multi-AZ (synchronous standby), read replicas (async), automated backups
  • RDS — Parameter Groups, Option Groups, Enhanced Monitoring, Performance Insights
  • RDS — IAM DB authentication, Secrets Manager rotation, SSL/TLS
  • RDS — storage autoscaling, gp3 storage, Provisioned IOPS
  • Aurora — cluster volume (shared, 6-way replication), writer + reader instances
  • Aurora — Aurora Serverless v2 — ACU scaling, warm capacity
  • Aurora — Global Database — cross-region, RPO < 1s, RTO < 1min
  • Aurora — Aurora I/O-Optimized — predictable I/O costs, aurora_io_read_throughput
  • Aurora — Fast Clone, Backtrack, parallel query
  • DynamoDB — partition key, sort key, composite key design
  • DynamoDB — RCU/WCU, OnDemand vs Provisioned, burst capacity
  • DynamoDB — LSI (same partition), GSI (different partition key), sparse indexes
  • DynamoDB — Transactions — TransactWriteItems, TransactGetItems, ACID
  • DynamoDB — DynamoDB Streams — NEW_IMAGE, OLD_IMAGE, KEYS_ONLY
  • DynamoDB — Accelerator (DAX) — write-through, eventual consistency cache
  • DynamoDB — single-table design — entity adjacency, overloading GSI
  • DynamoDB — TTL, ExpressionAttributeNames, ConditionExpression
  • DynamoDB — Global Tables — multi-region, multi-master, last-writer-wins
  • ElastiCache — Redis vs Memcached, cluster mode, replication groups
  • ElastiCache Redis — sorted sets, pub/sub, Lua scripts, WAIT, OBJECT ENCODING
  • MemoryDB for Redis — durable, Multi-AZ, transaction log
  • DocumentDB — MongoDB 5.0 compatibility, clusters, Change Streams
  • Keyspaces — serverless Cassandra, CQL, single-region/multi-region
  • Neptune — graph database, Gremlin, SPARQL, openCypher, bulk load
  • Timestream — time-series, MagneticStore/MemoryStore, scheduled queries
  • QLDB — ledger, immutable, PartiQL, SHA-256 verified journal

6. AWS Messaging & Integration

  • SQS — standard vs FIFO, visibility timeout, ReceiveMessageWaitTimeSeconds (long polling)
  • SQS — DLQ, maxReceiveCount, RedrivePolicy, DLQ redrive
  • SQS — message attributes, MessageGroupId, MessageDeduplicationId
  • SQS — large message payloads — S3 extended client, SQS_LARGE_PAYLOAD_SUPPORT
  • SNS — topics (standard/FIFO), subscriptions (SQS, Lambda, HTTP, email, SMS, mobile push)
  • SNS — MessageFilterPolicy, MessageAttributes, fan-out pattern
  • SNS — SSE, DLQ for Lambda/SQS subscribers
  • EventBridge — event buses (default, custom, partner), rules, targets
  • EventBridge — event patterns, detail-type, source, content filtering
  • EventBridge — Scheduler — one-time, cron, rate, FlexibleTimeWindow
  • EventBridge — Pipes — source → filter → enrichment → target
  • EventBridge — Schema Registry, schema discovery, code bindings
  • Kinesis Data Streams — shards, PartitionKey, SequenceNumber, retention (1–365 days)
  • Kinesis — GetRecords (classic) vs SubscribeToShard (enhanced fan-out), EFO
  • Kinesis — KCL, KPL, aggregation, PutRecords batching
  • Kinesis Data Firehose — delivery streams, BufferingHints, dynamic partitioning
  • Firehose — transformations (Lambda), format conversion (Parquet/ORC), S3 prefix
  • Kinesis Data Analytics — Flink, SQL, RANDOM_CUT_FOREST, anomaly detection
  • MSK (Managed Kafka) — broker types, tiered storage, MSK Connect, MSK Serverless
  • MQ — ActiveMQ, RabbitMQ managed brokers, network of brokers
  • Step Functions — Standard vs Express workflows, states (Task, Choice, Parallel, Map, Wait)
  • Step Functions — Catch, Retry, ResultPath, Parameters, ItemSelector
  • Step Functions — activity workers, Optimistic Locking, ExecutionList
  • AppSync — managed GraphQL, resolvers (VTL, JS), PIPELINE resolvers, subscriptions
  • API Gateway — REST vs HTTP vs WebSocket APIs, stages, throttling, usage plans
  • API Gateway — Lambda proxy integration, Lambda Authorizer, Cognito Authorizer

  • IAM — users, groups, roles, policies (identity-based, resource-based, permission boundary)

  • Cognito — JWT tokens (id, access, refresh), Triggers (Lambda), MFA
  • KMS — CMK (Customer Managed Key), aws managed, AWS owned

  • KMS — Automatic key rotation, Multi-Region Keys, Asymmetric Keys
  • Secrets Manager — automatic rotation, Lambda rotation function, SecretString

  • ACM — certificate provisioning, auto-renewal, DNS validation, EMAIL validation

  • GuardDuty — threat detection, findings (UnauthorizedAccess, Recon, CryptoCurrency)

  • Inspector — EC2/Lambda/ECR vulnerability scanning, CVSS scoring

  • CloudTrail — management events, data events, Insights, Event history, integrity validation
  • Detective — investigation, graph model, VPC Flow Logs correlation

19. Snowflake Architecture & Internals

  • Snowflake architecture — Cloud Services Layer, Query Processing Layer, Storage Layer
  • Virtual Warehouses (VW) — X-Small to 6X-Large, multi-cluster, maximized vs auto-scale
  • VW — AUTO_SUSPEND, AUTO_RESUME, INITIALLY_SUSPENDED, credit consumption
  • Multi-cluster warehouse — min/max clusters, ECONOMY vs MAXIMIZED scaling policy
  • Storage — columnar, compressed, micro-partitions, immutable, auto-clustered
  • Micro-partitions — 50–500MB compressed, metadata (min/max, null count, distinct count)
  • Clustering — CLUSTER BY, clustering keys, clustering depth, AUTOMATIC CLUSTERING
  • Time Travel — AT, BEFORE, DATA_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS (0–90 Enterprise)
  • Fail-Safe — 7-day recovery (non-user accessible), storage cost implications
  • Zero-Copy Cloning — CREATE TABLE … CLONE, metadata only, no data duplication
  • Data Sharing — SHARE, READER ACCOUNT, DATA EXCHANGE, Private Data Exchange
  • Data Sharing — Secure Views, SECURE UDF, listing governance
  • Snowpark — Python/Java/Scala DataFrames on Snowflake, pushdown execution
  • Snowpark — Session, DataFrame.collect(), to_pandas(), Column expressions
  • Snowpark Container Services (SPCS) — Docker containers, IMAGE REPOSITORY, SERVICE
  • SPCS — COMPUTE POOL, SERVICE FUNCTION, JOB SERVICE
  • Streams — STANDARD, APPEND-ONLY, INSERT_ONLY (external), SYSTEM$STREAM_HAS_DATA
  • Tasks — SCHEDULE, AFTER (DAG), WAREHOUSE or SERVERLESS, TASK_HISTORY
  • Task DAGs — directed acyclic graph, root task, branching, SUSPEND/RESUME
  • Dynamic Tables — TARGET_LAG, WAREHOUSE, incremental refresh, DOWNSTREAM
  • Materialized Views — automatic refresh, clustering support, limitations
  • Cortex — COMPLETE, CLASSIFY_TEXT, SENTIMENT, SUMMARIZE LLM functions
  • Cortex Analyst — semantic layer, natural language to SQL
  • Cortex Search — hybrid vector + keyword, SEARCH SERVICE
  • Hybrid Tables — transactional workloads, PRIMARY KEY, FOREIGN KEY, low latency reads
  • Iceberg Tables — external volume, CATALOG INTEGRATION, STORAGE INTEGRATION
  • Iceberg — Snowflake as catalog, external catalog (Glue, Polaris), CONVERT TO ICEBERG
  • Polaris Catalog — open-source Iceberg catalog, multi-engine, REST CATALOG API
  • Performance optimization — QUERY PROFILE, EXPLODING JOINS, spillage to disk
  • Performance — RESULT CACHE, LOCAL DISK CACHE, REMOTE DISK CACHE
  • RBAC — roles, privileges, GRANT, REVOKE, role hierarchy, SYSADMIN/SECURITYADMIN
  • Snowflake RBAC — ACCOUNTADMIN, SYSADMIN, SECURITYADMIN, USERADMIN, PUBLIC
  • Row Access Policies — POLICY_CONTEXT, dynamic filtering per role/user
  • Column Masking Policies — MASKING POLICY, USING, conditional unmasking
  • Tag-based governance — TAG, APPLY TAG, SYSTEM$GET_TAG_ALLOWED_VALUES
  • Network Policies — IP allowlist, blocklist, USER-level override
  • Private Connectivity — PRIVATELINK, PRIVATE SERVICE CONNECT, AWS PrivateLink
  • External Functions — API integration, REMOTE SERVICE, Lambda/APIM backend
  • External Stages — S3, Azure Blob, GCS, STORAGE INTEGRATION, FILE FORMAT
  • Snowpipe — AUTO_INGEST, PIPE, SQS/SNS notifications, Snowpipe Streaming
  • Snowpipe Streaming — SDK, insertRows, migrate, low latency ingest
  • Data Quality — DATA METRIC FUNCTION, SYSTEM$DATA_METRIC_FUNCTION_STATS
  • Replication — REPLICATION GROUP, FAILOVER GROUP, PRIMARY/SECONDARY
  • Snowflake on AWS/Azure/GCP — ACCOUNT LOCATOR, region groups, multi-cloud replication
  • INFORMATION_SCHEMA — QUERY_HISTORY, WAREHOUSE_METERING_HISTORY, STORAGE_USAGE
  • Account Usage — SNOWFLAKE.ACCOUNT_USAGE, 45-min latency, 1-year retention
  • Resource Monitors — credit quota, SUSPEND, SUSPEND_IMMEDIATE, NOTIFY
  • Cost management — QUERY_ACCELERATION_SERVICE, SEARCH OPTIMIZATION SERVICE

20. Databricks Architecture & Platform

  • Databricks architecture — Control Plane (Databricks-managed) vs Data Plane (customer VNet/VPC)
  • Workspace — Premium / Enterprise plans, Unity Catalog, workspace-level metastore
  • Unity Catalog — metastore, catalog, schema, table, volume, model — 3-level namespace
  • Unity Catalog — GRANT, REVOKE, securable objects, data lineage, audit logs
  • Unity Catalog — Delta Sharing, External Locations, Storage Credentials
  • Delta Lake — ACID transactions, _delta_log, JSON + Parquet, checkpoints
  • Delta — OPTIMIZE, ZORDER BY, VACUUM, RESTORE TO, time travel (VERSION AS OF, TIMESTAMP AS OF)
  • Delta — MERGE INTO, COPY INTO, CDC patterns, APPLY CHANGES INTO (DLT)
  • Delta — Change Data Feed (CDF) — _commit_timestamp, _change_type
  • Delta — Deletion Vectors, Liquid Clustering, Column Mapping
  • Delta — Predictive Optimization — automatic OPTIMIZE/VACUUM (Unity Catalog)
  • Delta Live Tables (DLT) — @dlt.table, @dlt.view, @dlt.expect, pipeline modes
  • DLT — COMPLETE vs APPEND vs SCD Type 1/2, APPLY CHANGES INTO
  • DLT — Development vs Production pipeline, Enhanced Autoscaling
  • Databricks SQL — SQL Warehouse (Classic/Pro/Serverless), Query History, Alerts
  • DBSQL — Dashboards (Lakeview), AI/BI Dashboard, Genie (NL to SQL)
  • Databricks Asset Bundles (DAB) — bundle.yml, databricks bundle deploy, CI/CD
  • Workflows — Job Clusters vs All-Purpose Clusters, tasks (notebook, Python, JAR, SQL, DLT, ML)
  • Workflows — repair run, conditional task execution, for-each task, task values
  • MLflow — Tracking, Projects, Models, Registry, Serving
  • MLflow — autolog, log_metric, log_artifact, log_model, transition_model_version
  • Feature Store / Feature Engineering — FeatureEngineeringClient, FeatureLookup
  • AutoML — databricks.automl.classify/regress/forecast, glass-box models
  • Model Serving — Serverless Real-Time Inference, Provisioned Throughput, External Models
  • Mosaic AI — Model Training, Foundation Model APIs, Agent Framework, Vector Search
  • Mosaic AI — RAG, Agent Evaluation, LangChain integration, llm/v1/chat endpoint
  • Photon — vectorized query engine, C++, Arrow, Delta and Parquet acceleration
  • Cluster configuration — DBR (Databricks Runtime), ML Runtime, GPU Runtime, Photon Runtime
  • Cluster — All-Purpose, Job Cluster, SQL Warehouse, Instance Pool
  • Cluster autoscaling — min/max workers, spark.databricks.aggressiveWindowDownS
  • Structured Streaming — readStream, writeStream, trigger, checkpointLocation
  • Structured Streaming — foreach batch, watermarks, stateful, deduplication
  • Apache Spark on Databricks — SparkSession, DataFrame, RDD (avoid), Dataset
  • Spark — transformations (lazy) vs actions (eager), DAG, Stage, Task
  • Spark — shuffle, broadcastJoin, skew join, AQE (Adaptive Query Execution)
  • Spark — Catalyst optimizer, Tungsten, code generation, whole-stage codegen
  • Spark — persist/cache, StorageLevel, unpersist, checkpointing
  • Spark — partitionBy, repartition, coalesce, bucketBy
  • Spark — spark.sql.shuffle.partitions, spark.sql.files.maxPartitionBytes
  • Spark Streaming — micro-batch, continuous processing, trigger(processingTime)
  • Databricks Connect — remote Spark execution from IDE, v2 (Python SDK)
  • DBFS (Databricks File System) — dbfs:/, /mnt/, Unity Catalog volumes (preferred)
  • Secrets — databricks secrets, dbutils.secrets.get, Secret Scope (Databricks/Azure KV)
  • init scripts — cluster-scoped, global, cluster_log_conf
  • Lakehouse architecture — Bronze/Silver/Gold medallion, Delta Lake as foundation
  • Lakehouse Federation — query external systems without ingestion, CREATE FOREIGN CATALOG
  • Marketplace — data products, notebook solutions, Delta Sharing based exchange

21. Data Lakehouse & Open Table Formats

  • Apache Iceberg — snapshot, manifest list, manifest file, data files
  • Iceberg — table spec v1/v2/v3, partition spec evolution, schema evolution
  • Iceberg — hidden partitioning, partition transforms (identity, year, month, day, hour, bucket, truncate)
  • Iceberg — merge-on-read vs copy-on-write, deletion vectors
  • Iceberg — catalog implementations — REST, Hive, Glue, JDBC, Nessie, Polaris
  • Iceberg — time travel, branching, tagging, WAP (Write-Audit-Publish)
  • Apache Hudi — COW vs MOR table types, timeline, COMPACTION, CLEAN
  • Hudi — Record Key, Partition Path, Precombine Field, upsert, incremental queries
  • Delta Lake — see §20 — _delta_log, ACID, DML, CDF, Liquid Clustering
  • Apache Parquet — row groups, column chunks, page encoding, predicate pushdown
  • Parquet — SNAPPY vs ZSTD vs GZIP, metadata, bloom filters
  • Apache ORC — stripe, index, bloom filter, Hive native format
  • Apache Avro — schema evolution, GenericRecord, SpecificRecord, Kafka registry
  • Apache Arrow — columnar in-memory, IPC, Flight, zero-copy, ADBC
  • Parquet + Iceberg vs Delta — engine compatibility, ecosystem, cloud support
  • Data Mesh — domain ownership, data as product, self-serve platform, federated governance
  • Medallion architecture — Bronze (raw), Silver (cleansed), Gold (aggregated/serving)
  • Lakehouse vs Data Warehouse vs Data Lake — trade-offs, ACID, BI support, ML support

22. Data Engineering & Pipeline Patterns

  • Apache Spark — SparkSession, DataFrame API, SQL, Streaming, MLlib
  • Spark optimization — AQE, DPP (Dynamic Partition Pruning), skew hint, broadcastJoin
  • Apache Kafka — producers, consumers, KStreams, KSQL, Kafka Connect, MirrorMaker 2
  • Kafka — exactly-once, idempotent producer, transactional API
  • Apache Flink — DataStream, Table API, SQL, stateful, checkpointing, savepoint
  • Flink — event time vs processing time, watermarks, windows (tumbling, sliding, session)
  • Apache Airflow — DAG, Operator, Sensor, XCom, TaskGroup, dynamic task mapping
  • Airflow — Executor (Local, Celery, Kubernetes), Connection, Variable, Pool
  • Airflow — Airflow on Kubernetes — KubernetesPodOperator, KubernetesExecutor
  • dbt (data build tool) — models, sources, seeds, snapshots, tests, macros
  • dbt — ref(), source(), incremental materialization, unique_key, on_schema_change
  • dbt — packages, exposures, metrics, semantic layer, dbt Cloud
  • Prefect — @flow, @task, deployments, ConcurrentTaskRunner, blocks
  • Dagster — @asset, @op, @job, IOManager, Partition, Software-Defined Assets
  • Dagster — Definitions, sensors, schedules, asset checks, metadata
  • CDC (Change Data Capture) — Debezium, log-based, query-based, outbox pattern
  • ELT vs ETL — modern lakehouse preference for ELT
  • Data contracts — schema registry, Avro/Protobuf/JSON Schema, breaking change detection
  • Batch vs Streaming — latency trade-offs, Lambda architecture, Kappa architecture
  • Schema evolution — backward/forward/full compatibility, Avro rules
  • Data quality — Great Expectations, Soda, dbt tests, Monte Carlo
  • Orchestration patterns — fan-out, fan-in, conditional branching, sensor patterns

23. Observability, Monitoring & Operations

  • Unified observability — metrics, logs, traces — AWS CloudWatch, Azure Monitor, Datadog
  • Datadog — Agent, APM, Logs, RUM, Synthetics, Infrastructure
  • Datadog — DDtrace, distributed tracing, Service Catalog, Monitors, SLOs
  • Grafana — dashboards, Loki (logs), Tempo (traces), Mimir (metrics), OnCall
  • OpenTelemetry — OTLP, Collector, Processor, Exporter, auto-instrumentation
  • Prometheus — PromQL, recording rules, alerting rules, AlertManager, Thanos
  • ELK / OpenSearch — Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana, Beats, ILM policies
  • Incident Management — PagerDuty, OpsGenie, escalation policies, on-call rotation
  • SLO/SLI/SLA — error budget, burn rate, CUJ (Critical User Journey)
  • Cost Management — AWS Cost Explorer, Azure Cost Management, Infracost, kubecost
  • DORA metrics — deployment frequency, lead time, MTTR, change failure rate
  • Chaos Engineering — AWS FIS, Azure Chaos Studio, Gremlin, Chaos Monkey
  • Runbooks — automated remediation, EventBridge → SSM Automation
  • Distributed tracing — W3C traceparent, B3, trace sampling, Jaeger, Zipkin
  • FinOps tooling — Spot.io, Cast.ai, Apptio Cloudability, tag compliance

24. Security, Compliance & Governance

  • Zero Trust Architecture — BeyondCorp, microsegmentation, device trust, identity-centric
  • CSPM — Cloud Security Posture Management — Defender for Cloud, AWS Security Hub
  • CWPP — Cloud Workload Protection — runtime protection, vulnerability scanning
  • CIEM — Cloud Infrastructure Entitlement Management — excess permissions, CloudKnox
  • Data Classification — Sensitivity Labels, Microsoft Purview, AWS Macie
  • Compliance frameworks — SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, FedRAMP
  • Encryption — at rest (AES-256), in transit (TLS 1.2+), in use (Confidential Computing)
  • Confidential Computing — AMD SEV, Intel TDX, Azure Confidential VMs, Nitro Enclaves
  • BYOK / HYOK / CMK — key ownership, Azure Key Vault Managed HSM, AWS CloudHSM
  • Network segmentation — micro-segmentation, NSG/SG, PrivateLink, VNet Service Endpoints
  • Identity Governance — access reviews, PIM, Entitlement Management, lifecycle workflows
  • DevSecOps — SAST, DAST, SCA, IaC scanning, Checkov, tfsec, trufflehog
  • Supply chain security — Sigstore, cosign, SBOM, SLSA framework
  • Audit logging — CloudTrail, Azure Activity Log, immutable audit, WORM
  • Vulnerability management — AWS Inspector, Azure Defender, Qualys, Tenable
  • Data residency — region restrictions, data sovereignty, Azure Policy, SCP

25. Staff / Principal Engineering Competencies

  • Cloud strategy — multi-cloud vs polycloud vs cloud-first, vendor risk
  • Architecture patterns — event-driven, data mesh, lakehouse, microservices on cloud
  • Technology evaluation — RFP process, PoC design, TCO analysis, trade-off documentation
  • RFC / ADR — cloud architecture decisions, migration justifications, platform choices
  • Platform engineering — internal developer platform, Backstage, golden paths, self-service
  • Landing zone design — account vending, AWS Control Tower, Azure Landing Zone
  • Cloud governance — tagging strategy, AWS Organizations, Azure Management Groups
  • FinOps program — budgets, showback/chargeback, unit cost metrics, optimization cycle
  • Migration strategy — Rehost, Replatform, Repurchase, Refactor, Retire, Retain (7Rs)
  • Migration tooling — AWS Migration Hub, Azure Migrate, Cloudamize, TSO Logic
  • DR strategy — Backup & Restore, Pilot Light, Warm Standby, Multi-Site Active-Active
  • BCP — RTO/RPO by tier, runbook automation, GameDay exercises
  • Security program — threat modeling, STRIDE, pen testing, red team exercises
  • Data platform strategy — lakehouse adoption, governance model, data catalog rollout
  • Well-Architected Review — conducting reviews, priorities, improvement plan
  • Cost optimization program — rightsizing cadence, reserved instance strategy, spot adoption
  • Hiring — cloud architecture rubrics, system design calibration
  • Mentorship — cloud learning paths, certification strategy (Solutions Architect Professional)
  • Incident management — Runbook automation, postmortem culture, chaos game days
  • Standards & guardrails — Service Control Policies, Azure Policy initiatives, compliance automation

Database Complete Interview Reference — Full Edition

Database Complete

1. Core Database Concepts

Fundamentals

  • Database → Organized collection of structured data stored and accessed electronically.
  • DBMS → Software managing storage, retrieval, and manipulation (MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle).
  • RDBMS → DBMS based on the relational model; tables with rows/columns; SQL interface.
  • NoSQL → Non-relational; flexible schema; horizontally scalable; key-value/document/graph/column stores.
  • Data Model → Abstract representation of how data is organized and related.
  • Schema → Blueprint defining structure (tables, columns, types, constraints). Logical blueprint of the DB.
  • Instance → Snapshot of actual data in the DB at a given moment (schema = type, instance = value).
  • Metadata → Data about data; describes structure, format, relationships.
  • Data Dictionary → Centralized metadata repository; tracks table names, column types, constraints.
  • Catalog → System-maintained metadata store (e.g. pg_catalog in PostgreSQL).
  • Logical vs Physical Model → Logical = entities/relationships (ER diagram); Physical = actual tables/indexes/storage.
  • Keywords: Schema vs Instance is like a class vs object; DBMS enforces schema; NoSQL may be schema-less or schema-flexible.

2. Data Models

Types of Data Models

  • Relational Model → Tables with rows/columns; relationships via foreign keys; basis of all RDBMS.
  • Hierarchical Model → Tree structure; parent-child; fast reads but rigid (IBM IMS legacy).
  • Network Model → Graph of records; many-to-many via set; predecessor to relational.
  • Document Model → Semi-structured JSON/BSON documents; flexible schema (MongoDB, CouchDB).
  • Key-Value Model → Simplest; key maps to opaque value; ultra-fast (Redis, DynamoDB).
  • Column-Family Model → Rows grouped into column families; sparse; write-optimized (Cassandra, HBase).
  • Graph Model → Nodes + edges; traversal queries; relationships are first-class (Neo4j, Neptune).
  • Time-Series Model → Optimized for timestamped data; append-heavy; time as primary index (InfluxDB, TimescaleDB).
  • Object-Oriented Database → Objects stored directly; rare; used in CAD/scientific apps.
  • Vector Model → Stores high-dimensional embeddings; optimized for similarity search (Pinecone, Weaviate).
  • Keywords: Document = flexible hierarchy; Column-family = analytical wide rows; Graph = relationship-heavy queries; Vector = AI/ML similarity.

3. Database Types

Classification by Workload & Architecture

  • OLTP → Online Transaction Processing; high-frequency short transactions; row-store; normalized (MySQL, PostgreSQL).
  • OLAP → Online Analytical Processing; complex aggregations over large datasets; column-store (Redshift, BigQuery).
  • HTAP → Hybrid Transactional/Analytical Processing; handles both workloads (TiDB, SingleStore).
  • In-Memory Database → Data stored in RAM; microsecond latency; volatile or persisted (Redis, VoltDB, SAP HANA).
  • Distributed Database → Data spread across nodes; horizontal scale; CAP trade-offs (CockroachDB, Spanner).
  • Embedded Database → Runs inside application process; no separate server (SQLite, H2, RocksDB).
  • Cloud Database → Managed by cloud provider; auto-scale, automated backups (RDS, Aurora, Cloud SQL).
  • Serverless Database → Scale-to-zero; pay-per-query; no server management (Aurora Serverless, Neon, PlanetScale).
  • NewSQL → SQL interface + horizontal scalability + ACID; addresses RDBMS scaling limits (CockroachDB, YugabyteDB).
  • Keywords: OLTP = many small writes; OLAP = few large reads; choose DB type based on read/write ratio and query patterns.

4. Data Types

Common Data Types Across Databases

  • Numeric → INT, BIGINT, DECIMAL(p,s), FLOAT, NUMERIC; choose precision carefully for money (use DECIMAL not FLOAT).
  • Character / String → CHAR(n) fixed-width, VARCHAR(n) variable; TEXT unlimited; CHAR pads, VARCHAR doesn't.
  • Boolean → BOOLEAN; true/false/null; stored as 1 byte in PostgreSQL.
  • Date / Time / Timestamp → DATE, TIME, TIMESTAMP, TIMESTAMPTZ (with timezone); always store in UTC.
  • Binary (BLOB) → Binary Large Object; store files/images; better to store path + object storage instead.
  • Text (CLOB) → Character Large Object; large text; full-text search applicable.
  • JSON / JSONB → Semi-structured; JSONB (PostgreSQL) is binary, indexed, queryable; JSON is text-stored.
  • Spatial / Geospatial → GEOMETRY, GEOGRAPHY; PostGIS extension; latitude/longitude; distance queries.
  • Array / Composite → PostgreSQL supports array columns and composite types natively.
  • UUID / GUID → 128-bit unique ID; globally unique; good for distributed primary keys (random UUIDs cause index fragmentation).
  • Vector / Embedding → Fixed-length float array; used for ML embeddings; pgvector extension in PostgreSQL.
  • Keywords: DECIMAL over FLOAT for money; TIMESTAMPTZ over TIMESTAMP; JSONB over JSON for querying; UUID v7 preserves time-ordering.

5. Database Objects

Core Objects

  • Table → Primary storage unit; rows + columns; lives inside a schema.
  • Row / Record → Single tuple; represents one entity instance.
  • Column / Field → Named attribute with a specific data type and constraints.
  • View → Virtual table from a stored SELECT; no own storage; simplifies queries; not always updatable.
  • Materialized View → Persisted view result; needs REFRESH; faster read but stale until refreshed.
  • Index → Auxiliary data structure for fast lookup; trades write/space overhead for read speed.
  • Sequence → Auto-incrementing number generator; used for surrogate keys.
  • Trigger → Automatic procedure on INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE; use sparingly (hidden logic, hard to debug).
  • Stored Procedure → Named reusable SQL block; runs on DB server; reduces network round-trips.
  • Function → Returns a value; can be used in SELECT; deterministic functions can be indexed.
  • Schema (namespace) → Logical container grouping DB objects; allows same table name in different schemas.
  • Tablespace → Physical storage location mapping; allows placing tables/indexes on different disks.
  • Partition → Logical division of a table; range/list/hash; transparent to queries.
  • Extension / Plugin → Additional capabilities (PostGIS, pgvector, pg_stat_statements).
  • Keywords: Materialized view = performance at cost of freshness; triggers = power but hidden coupling; stored procedures = server-side logic.

6. Relationships & Constraints

Keys

  • Primary Key → Unique + Not Null; one per table; clustered index by default in MySQL InnoDB.
  • Foreign Key → References PK of another table; enforces referential integrity; adds join overhead.
